title: Capability Buildprint Standard
creator: Agent Buildprint
summary: A draft v0 standard for packaging reusable agent-installable capabilities such as Stripe, RBAC, auth, analytics, admin UI, and deployment.
promise: Defines the first compact Capability Buildprint format so agents can add bounded powers to existing software without rediscovering implementation contracts from scratch.
includes: phased grafting protocol for existing host repos
host assessment and integration plan checkpoints
starter JSON schema for scanner/tool validation
risks: Draft standard needs real capability packets to harden edge cases
Scanner and registry UI do not yet deeply validate capability.yaml
Examples are contract examples, not full provider implementations
checks: Use examples to author one bounded capability packet
Do not claim runtime install success without verify.md proof

trustBadges.label: Draft standard
trustBadges.detail: First published shape for Capability Buildprints.

trustBadges.label: Composable
trustBadges.detail: Designed for bounded capabilities that can be applied to host projects.

publicStatus.label: Draft v0
publicStatus.explanation: Ready for dogfooding with real Stripe, RBAC, and auth capability packets.

whatYouGet: A compact file shape for reusable capability packets
A phased workflow for safely grafting capabilities into host repos
A manifest contract scanners and agents can parse
Apply, verify, compatibility, and composition rules
Concrete example manifests for billing and permissions
whatYouNeed: A target host family such as Next.js, Express, Supabase, or another framework
Concrete verification proof before claiming the capability works

howToUse.title: Author a capability
howToUse.detail: Copy the packet shape, fill capability.yaml, then write apply.md and verify.md for one bounded integration.

howToUse.title: Check compatibility
howToUse.detail: Use compatibility.md to declare host signals, expected existing capabilities, and composition rules.

howToUse.title: Dogfood it
howToUse.detail: Apply the packet to a real host repo and record proof level, blockers, and missing scanner rules.

resultChecklist: Secrets and side effects are declared
Apply instructions adapt to host architecture
Verification checks prove the capability, not just file creation
